title: An unexpected behavior in a query involving CTEs, DISTINCT ON, and JOIN
description:
### What happens?

I encountered a strange behavior from DuckDB. Let‚Äôs say I have this table:


```sql
CREATE TABLE log (
Login text,
Title text,
TS integer
);

INSERT INTO log(Login, Title, TS) VALUES
('kroiboi', 'rat', 1),
('missTiq', 'medic', 2),
('kroiboi', 'conman', 3),
('Joe', 'lordling', 4),
('Joe', 'meatball', 5),
('kroiboi', NULL, 100)
;
```

I‚Äôm trying to count entries (rows) for each Login, and JOIN the latest non-NULL Title for each user (Login), according to TS ‚Äî timestamps.

The following query produces the expected result in Postgres, but not in DuckDB.

```sql
WITH login_titles AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ON(Login) *
  FROM log
  WHERE Title IS NOT NULL
  ORDER BY Login, TS DESC
),
q AS (
  SELECT
  Login,
  count(*) AS reqs
  FROM log
  GROUP BY Login
)
SELECT q.reqs, login_titles.*
FROM q
JOIN login_titles
ON q.Login = login_titles.Login
ORDER BY reqs DESC
;
```

Expected (PostgreSQL 17.4):
```
 reqs |  login  |  title   | ts
------+---------+----------+----
    3 | kroiboi | conman   |  3
    2 | Joe     | meatball |  5
    1 | missTiq | medic    |  2
```

DuckDB:
```
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ reqs  ‚îÇ  Login  ‚îÇ  Title   ‚îÇ  TS   ‚îÇ
‚îÇ int64 ‚îÇ varchar ‚îÇ varchar  ‚îÇ int32 ‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î§
‚îÇ     2 ‚îÇ Joe     ‚îÇ meatball ‚îÇ     5 ‚îÇ
‚îÇ     1 ‚îÇ missTiq ‚îÇ medic    ‚îÇ     2 ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¥‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¥‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¥‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
```

Both CTEs by themselves (`q` and `login_titles`) produce comparable and expected results, both in Postgres and in DuckDB:

`login_titles`:
```
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ  Login  ‚îÇ  Title   ‚îÇ  TS   ‚îÇ
‚îÇ varchar ‚îÇ varchar  ‚îÇ int32 ‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î§
‚îÇ Joe     ‚îÇ meatball ‚îÇ     5 ‚îÇ
‚îÇ kroiboi ‚îÇ conman   ‚îÇ     3 ‚îÇ
‚îÇ missTiq ‚îÇ medic    ‚îÇ     2 ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¥‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¥‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
```

`q`:
```
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ  Login  ‚îÇ reqs  ‚îÇ
‚îÇ varchar ‚îÇ int64 ‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î§
‚îÇ Joe     ‚îÇ     2 ‚îÇ
‚îÇ kroiboi ‚îÇ     3 ‚îÇ
‚îÇ missTiq ‚îÇ     1 ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¥‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
```

But DuckDB drops ‚Äúkroiboi‚Äù row when those are JOIN-ed. Is this a bug or am I missing something?





### To Reproduce

.

### OS:

Linux 6.12.23-1-lts

### DuckDB Version:

DuckDB v1.2.2 7c039464e4

### DuckDB Client:

duckdb CLI

### Hardware:

### Full Name:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (UserID SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, Username text NOT NULL, CreatedAt timestamp);
INSERT INTO users(UserID, Username, CreatedAt) VALUES (1, 'alice', '2023-01-01 10:00:00'), (2, 'bob', '2023-01-02 11:00:00'), (3, 'charlie', '2023-01-03 12:00:00');
CREATE TABLE orders (OrderID SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, UserID integer, Amount decimal(10, 2), OrderDate timestamp, FOREIGN KEY (UserID) REFERENCES users(UserID));

WITH latest_orders AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON(UserID) * FROM orders ORDER BY UserID, OrderDate DESC) SELECT u.Username, lo.Amount FROM users u JOIN latest_orders lo ON u.UserID = lo.UserID WHERE lo.Amount IS NOT NULL ORDER BY u.Username;
key_bug_features: JOIN with DISTINCT ON and CTEs drops expected rows
